name: Leh – Ladakh
description: Leh is the gateway to the high-altitude cold desert of Ladakh, one of India's most dramatic landscapes. Situated at 3,500 m, it draws adventure travellers, bikers, and spiritual seekers with its Buddhist monasteries, turquoise lakes, and stark Himalayan passes.
shortPitch: Leh is the spectacular gateway to the high-altitude cold desert of Ladakh, a dramatic landscape of stark Himalayan passes and turquoise lakes. At 3,500m, it captivates adventure travellers and spiritual seekers alike with its ancient Buddhist monasteries.
topHighlights: Pangong Tso Lake and Nubra Valley excursions
Khardung La Pass, one of the world's highest motorable roads
Ancient Buddhist monasteries including Thiksey and Hemis

name: Nubra Valley
description: Accessible via the legendary Khardung La pass, Nubra Valley is a serene high-altitude desert valley where sand dunes meet snow-capped peaks. The Shyok and Nubra rivers converge here, and double-humped Bactrian camels roam the dunes — an almost surreal landscape.
shortPitch: Accessible via the legendary Khardung La pass, Nubra Valley is a serene high-altitude desert where sand dunes meet snow-capped peaks. Explore an almost surreal landscape where the Shyok and Nubra rivers converge amidst double-humped Bactrian camels.
topHighlights: Hunder sand dunes and Bactrian camel rides
Diskit Monastery and giant Maitreya Buddha statue
Panamik hot springs and Turtuk village

name: Zanskar Valley
description: One of India's most remote and dramatic valleys, Zanskar is accessible for only a few months each year when roads are open. In winter the legendary Chadar Trek takes adventurers across the frozen Zanskar River through gorges of extraordinary beauty. A magnet for serious trekkers and Buddhist pilgrims.
shortPitch: Zanskar is one of India's most remote and dramatic valleys, opening its roads to the world for only a few fleeting months each year. It is a legendary magnet for serious trekkers and Buddhist pilgrims, offering the extraordinary frozen Chadar Trek in winter.
topHighlights: Winter Chadar Trek across the frozen Zanskar River
Phugtal Monastery built into a cliffside cave
Thrilling white-water rafting on the Zanskar River

name: Spiti Valley
description: Spiti — the 'Middle Land' — is a high-altitude cold desert valley at 3,800 m, dotted with ancient Buddhist monasteries perched on clifftops. The lunar landscape, prayer flags, and crystal-clear skies make it one of India's most photogenic and spiritually resonant destinations.
shortPitch: Known as the 'Middle Land', Spiti is a high-altitude cold desert valley dotted with ancient Buddhist monasteries perched on dramatic clifftops. Its lunar landscape, fluttering prayer flags, and crystal-clear skies create a profoundly photogenic and spiritual atmosphere.
topHighlights: Key Monastery and Kibber village exploration
The pristine waters of Chandratal Lake
Hikkim, home to the world's highest post office

name: Manali
description: Manali is the most popular hill station in Himachal Pradesh and a major hub for adventure activities. Sitting at the head of the Kullu Valley, it offers skiing, paragliding, white-water rafting and trekking, alongside the old Manali village with its apple orchards and colonial charm.
shortPitch: Sitting at the head of the Kullu Valley, Manali is Himachal's premier hub for thrilling mountain adventures. From skiing and paragliding to the colonial charm and apple orchards of Old Manali, it perfectly balances adrenaline and alpine serenity.
topHighlights: Rohtang Pass excursions and snow activities
Solang Valley skiing and zorbing
Old-growth deodar forests at Hadimba Temple

name: Kasol & Kheerganga
description: Kasol is a tiny hamlet in the Parvati Valley that has become a cult backpacker destination, beloved for its Israeli cafes, riverside campsites and proximity to trekking trails. Kheerganga is the most popular overnight trek from Kasol, ending at natural hot springs with stunning valley views.
shortPitch: Kasol is a cult backpacker hamlet in the Parvati Valley, famous for its riverside campsites and thriving Israeli cafe culture. It serves as the base for the iconic Kheerganga trek, rewarding hikers with natural hot springs overlooking stunning mountain vistas.
topHighlights: Kheerganga Trek culminating in natural hot springs
Parvati River camping and cafe-hopping
Exploration of the ancient Malana village

name: Tosh & Khajjiar
description: Tosh is a remote Himalayan village near Kasol with sweeping glacier views and cosy guesthouses. Khajjiar, often called the 'Mini Switzerland of India', is a lush green meadow ringed by dense pine forests at 2,000 m above sea level, perfect for picnics and horseback rides.
shortPitch: Discover Tosh, a remote Himalayan village offering sweeping glacier views, and Khajjiar, celebrated as the 'Mini Switzerland of India'. These destinations combine lush green meadows ringed by dense pines with pristine, offbeat mountain trails.
topHighlights: Sweeping views of Tosh Glacier
Picnics and horseback riding at Khajjiar Lake meadow
Sunset viewpoints over the dramatic Dhauladhar range

name: Jibhi & Tirthan Valley
description: Jibhi and the Tirthan Valley are off-the-radar Himalayan gems offering wooden guesthouses, crystal trout streams and easy forest trails. The Great Himalayan National Park — a UNESCO World Heritage Site — begins here, sheltering snow leopards, Himalayan brown bears and over 375 bird species.
shortPitch: Jibhi and the Tirthan Valley are serene, off-the-radar Himalayan gems defined by crystal trout streams and traditional wooden architecture. Serving as the gateway to the Great Himalayan National Park, they offer unmatched tranquility and wildlife trekking.
topHighlights: Trekking in the UNESCO Great Himalayan National Park
Tirthan River fly-fishing for rainbow trout
Scenic hikes to Jalori Pass and Serolsar Lake

name: Dharamshala & McLeod Ganj
description: Home of the Dalai Lama and the Tibetan government-in-exile, McLeod Ganj is a vibrant spiritual town beneath the Dhauladhar range. It blends Tibetan Buddhism, colonial-era architecture and lively cafes into one of India's most cosmopolitan hill towns, beloved by backpackers and spiritual travellers alike.
shortPitch: As the home of the Dalai Lama, McLeod Ganj is a vibrant spiritual sanctuary nestled beautifully beneath the Dhauladhar range. It seamlessly blends Tibetan Buddhism, colonial-era architecture, and a lively cosmopolitan cafe culture.
topHighlights: Visits to the Namgyal Monastery and Dalai Lama's temple
The scenic and rewarding Triund Trek
Tibetan arts at the Norbulingka Institute

name: Bir Billing
description: Bir Billing is acclaimed as one of the world's best paragliding sites, regularly hosting the Paragliding World Cup. The Tibetan colony at Bir gives the village a distinct character with monasteries and cafes, while the take-off site at Billing (2,400 m) affords breathtaking views of the Kangra Valley.
shortPitch: Acclaimed globally as a premier paragliding destination, Bir Billing offers breathtaking flights over the lush Kangra Valley. The village below thrives with a distinct Tibetan colony, blending extreme adventure with deep monastic peace.
topHighlights: Tandem and solo paragliding from the Billing take-off site
Exploring the Bir Tibetan Colony and Sherab Ling Monastery
Mountain cycling and scenic meadow camping

name: Dalhousie
description: A charming Victorian-era hill station established by the British in 1854, Dalhousie offers colonial bungalows, snow-dusted pine forests and panoramic Himalayan vistas. It retains a peaceful, old-world character that sets it apart from busier Himachali resorts.
shortPitch: Established by the British in 1854, Dalhousie is a charming Victorian-era hill station wrapped in snow-dusted pine forests. It retains a peaceful, old-world character that offers panoramic Himalayan vistas away from the bustling crowds.
topHighlights: Hikes to Dainkund Peak for sweeping views
Walks through the Kalatop Wildlife Sanctuary
Colonial heritage walks around Gandhi Chowk

name: Auli
description: Auli is India's premier ski destination, offering 500 m of ski runs between 2,500 m and 3,049 m with stunning views of Nanda Devi and the Garhwal Himalayas. In summer it transforms into a lush meadow base for treks into the Valley of Flowers and Kuari Pass.
shortPitch: Auli is India's premier alpine ski destination, boasting dramatic runs and sweeping views of Nanda Devi. When the snow melts, it transforms into a lush meadow base perfect for trekking deep into the Garhwal Himalayas.
topHighlights: Winter skiing and snowboarding
Riding India's longest ropeway for panoramic views
Trekking to Kuari Pass and Gorson Bugyal meadow

name: Chopta & Tungnath
description: Dubbed the 'Mini Switzerland of Uttarakhand', Chopta is a serene alpine meadow leading to Tungnath — the world's highest Shiva temple at 3,680 m — and the Chandrashila peak with 360° Himalayan panoramas. Far less crowded than Kedarnath or Badrinath, it remains an unspoiled gem.
shortPitch: Dubbed the 'Mini Switzerland of Uttarakhand', Chopta is a pristine alpine meadow that leads to the world's highest Shiva temple. Free from heavy crowds, it rewards trekkers with breathtaking 360-degree panoramas of the Himalayas.
topHighlights: Trekking to the ancient Tungnath Temple
Summiting Chandrashila for views of Nanda Devi
Spring hikes through vibrant rhododendron forests

name: Rishikesh
description: The Yoga Capital of the World, Rishikesh sits where the Ganges descends from the Himalayas onto the plains. It is both the world's most famous yoga retreat hub and India's adventure sports capital — offering white-water rafting, bungee jumping, trekking and cliff jumping alongside ashrams and morning Ganga aarti.
shortPitch: As the global capital of Yoga, Rishikesh is a profound spiritual retreat nestled where the Ganges flows from the Himalayas. It perfectly bridges deep meditation and ashram life with adrenaline-pumping river rafting and bungee jumping.
topHighlights: Grade III-IV white-water rafting on the Ganges
Attending the mesmerising evening Ganga Aarti
Yoga retreats and exploring the historic Beatles Ashram

name: Valley of Flowers
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, the Valley of Flowers bursts into a riot of colour each monsoon (July–September) as over 500 species of wildflowers carpet the high-altitude meadow at 3,658 m. Combined with the Hemkund Sahib pilgrimage, it is one of India's most enchanting Himalayan journeys.
shortPitch: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, this high-altitude meadow bursts into a spectacular riot of colour during the monsoon. Blanketed by over 500 species of wildflowers, it remains one of the most enchanting and pristine treks in the Himalayas.
topHighlights: Witnessing the monsoon wildflower bloom (Jul-Sep)
Trekking to the sacred Hemkund Sahib Gurudwara
Spotting rare Himalayan flora and fauna

name: Kashmir Valley
description: The 'Paradise on Earth', Kashmir Valley is one of the most beautiful places in Asia. Its Dal Lake houseboats, saffron fields, Mughal gardens, apple orchards and snow-covered peaks make it a destination of legendary appeal. The Valley has an almost unmatched diversity of landscapes, from alpine meadows to tulip gardens.
shortPitch: Legendary as the 'Paradise on Earth', the Kashmir Valley offers an almost unmatched diversity of alpine landscapes. Its tranquil houseboats, vibrant Mughal gardens, and snow-covered peaks have drawn travellers for centuries.
topHighlights: Staying on a traditional Dal Lake houseboat
Strolling the historic Shalimar and Nishat Mughal Gardens
Exploring saffron fields and tulip gardens

name: Tawang
description: Tawang is home to the 400-year-old Tawang Monastery — the largest in India and the second-largest in the world after Lhasa — perched at 3,048 m with breathtaking views of the Himalayan ranges. The town is also significant as the birthplace of the 6th Dalai Lama and a living example of Tibetan Buddhist culture on Indian soil.
shortPitch: Perched at 3,048m, Tawang is a deeply spiritual frontier town home to India's largest and the world's second-largest Tibetan Buddhist monastery. It offers breathtaking Himalayan vistas and stands as a living testament to vibrant Tibetan culture on Indian soil.
topHighlights: The majestic 400-year-old Tawang Monastery
Crossing the dramatic Sela Pass and Sela Lake
Exploring the historic Bum La Pass at the India-China border

name: Ziro Valley
description: Ziro is a UNESCO World Heritage-listed valley renowned for its terraced paddy fields, pine hills and the unique Apatani tribal culture. The annual Ziro Music Festival draws indie music lovers from across Asia, making it India's most atmospheric open-air music event, set against a backdrop of misty mountains.
shortPitch: Ziro Valley is a UNESCO-listed haven renowned for its terraced paddy fields, mist-covered pine hills, and the unique Apatani tribal culture. Every September, it transforms into Asia's most atmospheric indie music destination with the renowned Ziro Music Festival.
topHighlights: Immersive experiences with the indigenous Apatani tribe
The spectacular outdoor Ziro Music Festival (September)
Trekking in the Talley Valley Wildlife Sanctuary

name: Gangtok
description: The capital of Sikkim, Gangtok is a vibrant hill city with Kanchenjunga — the world's third-highest peak — as its backdrop. It serves as the gateway to North Sikkim's remote high-altitude lakes, the revered Rumtek Monastery and the many trekking routes that wind through the Eastern Himalayas.
shortPitch: Gangtok is the vibrant capital of Sikkim, perched dramatically with sweeping views of Kanchenjunga, the world's third-highest peak. It perfectly balances a thriving mountain-city culture with access to deep Eastern Himalayan treks and revered monasteries.
topHighlights: The stunning high-altitude Tsomgo (Changu) Lake
Visits to the revered Rumtek Monastery
Nathula Pass at the historic India-China border

name: Yumthang Valley
description: Yumthang — the 'Valley of Flowers of Sikkim' — lies at 3,564 m in North Sikkim and blooms with rhododendrons and primulas from March to May. The Zero Point beyond Yumthang at 4,800 m is the last accessible point near the Chinese border, offering spectacular snow and glacier views.
shortPitch: Known as the 'Valley of Flowers of Sikkim', Yumthang sits at 3,564m and explodes with vibrant rhododendrons every spring. Further up lies Zero Point, a spectacular glacial frontier offering panoramic snowy vistas near the border.
topHighlights: Rhododendron sanctuary spring blooms (Mar-May)
Glacier views from Zero Point (4,800m)
Natural hot springs amidst alpine terrain

name: Kargil
description: Kargil is the second-largest town in Ladakh, a strategic military hub known worldwide for the 1999 Kargil War. Today it draws history and adventure travellers en route between Leh and Srinagar, with access to remote valleys, ancient cave Buddhist monasteries and the towering Zoji La pass.
shortPitch: Kargil is a deeply historic military hub set against a backdrop of stark, towering mountain passes. It acts as a rugged stepping stone to remote valleys, ancient cave monasteries, and the breathtaking Zoji La pass.
topHighlights: Paying respects at the Kargil War Memorial in Dras
Exploring the ancient Mulbekh Monastery rock sculptures
Discovering the remote glaciers of Suru Valley

name: Kinnaur Valley
description: Kinnaur is famed for its apple orchards, terraced vineyards, ancient wooden temples and the stunning confluence of the Sutlej River with Himalayan peaks. The Hindustan–Tibet Highway passes through here, offering dramatic cliff-hanging roads, landslide-prone passes and a transition from Hindu to Buddhist culture.
shortPitch: Kinnaur Valley is a cultural bridge between Hinduism and Buddhism, famed for its terraced apple orchards and ancient wooden temples. The dramatic Hindustan-Tibet Highway cuts through its cliff-hanging passes, offering an unforgettable adventure.
topHighlights: Chitkul, the last Indian village on the Indo-Tibet border
The ancient Bhimakali Temple in Sarahan
Spectacular views of the Kinnaur Kailash peak

name: North Goa
description: North Goa is India's most famous beach destination — a heady mix of Portuguese colonial heritage, lively beach shacks, water sports, flea markets and an infectious party scene. Beaches from Calangute to Vagator each have distinct personalities ranging from family-friendly to bohemian.
shortPitch: North Goa is India's premier coastal playground, offering a heady mix of Portuguese colonial heritage and an infectious party scene. Its legendary beaches feature lively shacks, water sports, and iconic flea markets.
topHighlights: Vibrant nightlife and the Anjuna Flea Market
Water sports at Baga and Calangute beaches
Sunset views from the cliff-top Fort Aguada and Chapora Fort

name: South Goa
description: South Goa is quieter and more upscale than the north, with long empty stretches of white sand, luxury resorts, ancient spice plantations and the old Portuguese capital of Margao. Palolem's crescent-shaped bay and Agonda's pristine sands are among India's most beautiful beaches.
shortPitch: Quieter and more upscale than its northern counterpart, South Goa boasts long, empty stretches of pristine white sand and luxury resorts. It is a tranquil retreat surrounded by ancient spice plantations and grand Portuguese cathedrals.
topHighlights: The stunning crescent-shaped bay of Palolem Beach
UNESCO-listed churches and basilicas of Old Goa
Day trips to the majestic Dudhsagar Waterfalls

name: Gokarna
description: Gokarna is a small temple town on the Karnataka coast that has evolved into a beloved backpacker beach destination. The pristine Om Beach — shaped like the Om symbol — and the quieter Half Moon and Paradise beaches attract travellers seeking a more authentic alternative to Goa, while the Mahabaleshwar Temple draws Hindu pilgrims.
shortPitch: Gokarna is a deeply spiritual coastal temple town that has evolved into a beloved, laid-back backpacker haven. Its pristine, symbol-shaped beaches offer a quieter, highly authentic alternative to the bustle of Goa.
topHighlights: Beach-hopping between Om, Half Moon, and Paradise beaches
Pilgrimage to the ancient Mahabaleshwar Temple
Serene sunset viewpoints from the Gokarna Fort cliffs

name: Andaman Islands
description: The Andaman Islands are India's premier tropical island destination, boasting some of Asia's finest snorkelling and diving over pristine coral reefs. Port Blair is the gateway, while Havelock (Swaraj Dweep) and Neil (Shaheed Dweep) islands offer luminescent beaches of global renown.
shortPitch: The Andaman Islands stand as India's premier tropical archipelago, boasting luminescent white-sand beaches and world-class scuba diving. Its pristine coral reefs and dense mangrove forests create an unparalleled oceanic escape.
topHighlights: Relaxing on Radhanagar Beach, consistently rated Asia's best
Scuba diving over pristine coral reefs at Havelock and Neil Islands
Historical tours of the Cellular Jail National Memorial

name: Lakshadweep
description: India's only coral atoll archipelago in the Arabian Sea, Lakshadweep's 36 islands offer crystal-clear lagoons, vibrant coral reefs and an almost untouched marine ecosystem. Strict visitor limits have kept it remarkably pristine. Agatti Island has the main airport; Bangaram is a luxury resort island.
shortPitch: As India's only coral atoll archipelago, Lakshadweep offers a hyper-exclusive island escape with crystal-clear lagoons and an untouched marine ecosystem. Strict visitor limits have preserved its staggering natural beauty.
topHighlights: Snorkelling and scuba diving in vibrant, untouched coral reefs
Luxury stays on the secluded Bangaram Island
Glass-bottom boat rides in the turquoise Agatti lagoon

name: Varkala
description: Varkala is famous for its dramatic red cliffs dropping onto a golden beach — a setting unlike anywhere else in India. The clifftop North Cliff promenade is lined with yoga studios, Ayurveda centres, and beach restaurants. The ancient Janardhana Swami Temple adds a spiritual dimension to this unique coastal destination.
shortPitch: Varkala is visually dramatic, famous for its towering red cliffs that drop sheer into the Arabian Sea. It is a soulful coastal retreat blending clifftop yoga, Ayurvedic healing, and ancient temple spirituality.
topHighlights: Sunset views from the bustling North Cliff promenade
Ayurveda wellness retreats and beachfront yoga
Visiting the ancient Janardhana Swami Temple

name: Karwar & Dandeli
description: Karwar is a pristine harbour town at the mouth of the Kali River on the northern Karnataka coast, offering unspoilt beaches largely untouched by mass tourism. Inland, Dandeli is Karnataka's adventure capital — a tiger reserve and white-water rafting hotspot on the Western Ghats rivers.
shortPitch: Combining untouched coastal beauty with deep forest adventure, this twin destination offers the best of both worlds. Enjoy pristine harbour beaches in Karwar before heading inland to Dandeli for white-water rafting and tiger safaris.
topHighlights: White-water rafting on the Kali River in Dandeli
Wildlife safaris spotting elusive black panthers
Unspoiled beach retreats at Devbagh and Kurumgad Island

name: Ratnagiri
description: Ratnagiri on the Konkan coast is the birthplace of Bal Gangadhar Tilak and home to India's most prized Alphonso mangoes. Its red-laterite cliffs, ancient forts, pristine coves and Ganesh ghat make it a scenic and historically rich coastal destination largely undiscovered by international travellers.
shortPitch: Ratnagiri is an undiscovered gem on the Konkan coast, defined by dramatic red-laterite cliffs and pristine ocean coves. Historically rich, it is also globally renowned as the heartland of the prized Alphonso mango.
topHighlights: Tours of organic Alphonso mango farms
Exploring Ratnadurg Fort and its historic lighthouse
Discovering the royal Thibaw Palace Museum

name: Jaipur
description: The 'Pink City', Jaipur is the gateway to Rajasthan and one of India's most visited destinations. Its rose-pink buildings, magnificent forts, Mughal-Rajput palaces and vibrant bazaars combine to make it a UNESCO World Heritage-listed city of extraordinary colour and royal grandeur.
shortPitch: The famed 'Pink City' is a UNESCO World Heritage marvel of royal grandeur and extraordinary colour. Jaipur enchants visitors with its majestic hilltop forts, opulent Mughal-Rajput palaces, and bustling artisan bazaars.
topHighlights: The colossal Amber Fort and its intricate Mirror Palace
The iconic, lattice-windowed Hawa Mahal (Palace of Winds)
The astronomical wonders of the Jantar Mantar observatory

name: Udaipur
description: Udaipur — the 'City of Lakes' — is widely considered India's most romantic city, its white marble palaces reflected in shimmering lakes against the backdrop of the Aravalli Hills. The Lake Palace hotel, appearing to float on Lake Pichola, is among the most photographed buildings in Asia.
shortPitch: Widely considered India's most romantic destination, Udaipur is a stunning 'City of Lakes' framed by the Aravalli Hills. Its ethereal white marble palaces appear to float magically upon shimmering, serene waters.
topHighlights: Romantic boat rides across the serene Lake Pichola
Exploring the massive, ornate City Palace complex
Sunset views from the hilltop Sajjangarh (Monsoon Palace)

name: Jodhpur
description: The 'Blue City', Jodhpur is dominated by the colossal Mehrangarh Fort rising 122 m from a rocky outcrop. The old city below is painted indigo blue, creating an iconic visual unlike any other in India. It serves as the western gateway to the Thar Desert and a hub for desert safaris and textile shopping.
shortPitch: The famed 'Blue City' of Jodhpur is visually iconic, defined by its sea of indigo houses resting beneath the colossal Mehrangarh Fort. It acts as the majestic western gateway to the Thar Desert, offering a rich tapestry of history and artisanal crafts.
topHighlights: The colossal Mehrangarh Fort and Museum
Wandering the vibrant Blue City old town
Shopping for spices and textiles at Sardar Market

name: Jaisalmer
description: The 'Golden City', Jaisalmer rises from the Thar Desert like a mirage — its honey-coloured sandstone fort and havelis (merchant mansions) glowing at sunset. It is India's quintessential desert city: camel safaris into the sand dunes of Sam and Khuri, folk music under the stars and intricately carved Jain temples await.
shortPitch: Rising like a mirage from the Thar Desert, the 'Golden City' of Jaisalmer glows beautifully at sunset from its honey-coloured sandstone fort. It is India's quintessential desert experience, offering starry camel safaris and intricately carved living forts.
topHighlights: Exploring the UNESCO-listed, living Jaisalmer Fort
Camel safaris through the Sam Sand Dunes
Intricately carved merchant mansions like Patwon Ki Haveli

name: Bikaner
description: Bikaner is a fascinating walled desert city famed for its medieval fort, ornate Jain havelis, camel breeding farm, and the extraordinary Karni Mata Temple (Rat Temple) at Deshnok. It is less touristed than Jaipur or Jodhpur but equally rich in royal architecture and desert heritage.
shortPitch: Bikaner is a deeply authentic walled desert city boasting exquisite royal architecture and ornate Jain havelis without the heavy crowds. It offers unique cultural sites like a national camel breeding farm and the extraordinary Karni Mata Temple.
topHighlights: The magnificent Junagarh Fort complex
The extraordinary Karni Mata Rat Temple at Deshnok
Guided camel encounters at the National Research Centre on Camel

name: Bundi
description: Bundi is one of Rajasthan's best-kept secrets — a small medieval town with an impossibly photogenic palace covered in Rajput murals, a clifftop fort and step-wells (baoris) of exquisite craftsmanship. Rudyard Kipling lived here and wrote much of 'Kim' in the town. Backpackers adore it for its authenticity.
shortPitch: One of Rajasthan's best-kept secrets, Bundi is a medieval town renowned for its impossibly photogenic palace, exquisite Rajput murals, and grand step-wells. It retains an unspoiled authenticity that inspired writers like Rudyard Kipling.
topHighlights: The intricate Chitrashala murals at Bundi Palace
Historic step-wells including the grand Raniji ki Baori
Hikes up to the clifftop Taragarh Fort

name: Pushkar
description: Pushkar is one of Hinduism's holiest cities, built around the sacred Pushkar Lake with 52 bathing ghats and the rare Brahma Temple (one of very few in the world). Every November it hosts the Pushkar Camel Fair — the world's largest — a spectacular spectacle of thousands of camels, horses and cattle.
shortPitch: Built around a deeply sacred lake with 52 bathing ghats, Pushkar is an ancient holy city renowned for its rare Brahma Temple. It is world-famous for its spectacular annual Camel Fair that transforms the desert into a riot of colour and energy.
topHighlights: Visiting the sacred Brahma Temple
Attending sunset rituals at the Pushkar Lake ghats
Experiencing the vibrant Pushkar Camel Fair (November)

name: Shekhawati Region
description: Shekhawati is an open-air gallery of some of the finest painted havelis in the world, created by wealthy merchant families in the 18th–19th centuries. The towns of Mandawa, Nawalgarh, Fatehpur and Ramgarh contain thousands of buildings adorned with extraordinary frescoes — mythological, historical and fantastical.
shortPitch: Shekhawati is an extraordinary open-air gallery of ornate painted havelis commissioned by wealthy 18th-century merchants. Towns like Mandawa and Nawalgarh are adorned with thousands of vibrant frescoes depicting mythological and historical tales.
topHighlights: Exploring the painted havelis of Mandawa
The Nawalgarh fresco architectural trail
Discovering the grand cenotaphs of Ramgarh

name: Mysore
description: Mysore — 'City of Palaces' — is famous for the stunning Mysore Palace, one of India's most visited buildings. It is the cultural capital of Karnataka, home to silk weaving, sandalwood crafts and a living Wadiyar royal tradition. The Dasara festival transforms Mysore into one of India's grandest royal spectacles.
shortPitch: Known as the 'City of Palaces', Mysore is Karnataka's regal cultural capital celebrated for silk, sandalwood, and a vibrant Wadiyar royal tradition. The spectacularly illuminated Mysore Palace is a breathtaking architectural marvel.
topHighlights: The stunningly illuminated Mysore Palace
Ascending Chamundi Hill to the Chamundeshwari Temple
Exploring traditional silk and spice stalls at Devaraja Market

name: Rann of Kutch
description: The Great Rann of Kutch is one of the world's largest salt deserts — a vast white expanse stretching 7,500 sq km that becomes submerged in monsoon and emerges as a spectral white plain under the full moon in winter. The Rann Utsav festival (Oct–Feb) celebrates the region's tribal crafts, folk music and flamingo colonies.
shortPitch: The Great Rann of Kutch is a vast, spectral white salt desert stretching endlessly towards the horizon. Emerging in winter, it hosts the vibrant Rann Utsav festival celebrating rich tribal crafts and folk music under a full moon.
topHighlights: Experiencing the vast White Rann on a full moon night
Attending the cultural Rann Utsav festival
Exploring the ancient Harappan ruins at Dholavira

name: Alleppey (Alappuzha)
description: Alleppey — the 'Venice of the East' — is the houseboat capital of India. Its intricate network of canals, lagoons and lakes formed by the Kerala Backwaters is navigated by traditional rice-barge houseboats (kettuvallams), offering one of the most unique and tranquil travel experiences in Asia.
shortPitch: Often called the 'Venice of the East', Alleppey is the undisputed houseboat capital of India. Cruising its intricate, lush network of canals and lagoons on a traditional rice-barge offers unparalleled tropical tranquility.
topHighlights: Overnight stays on traditional Kerala houseboats
Navigating the serene backwater canals
Witnessing the spectacular Nehru Trophy Snake Boat Race (August)

name: Varanasi
description: Varanasi is one of the world's oldest continuously inhabited cities and Hinduism's most sacred city. The ghats of the Ganga are a theatre of human existence — morning yoga, boat rides past burning ghats, temple rituals, sadhus and the evening Ganga Aarti fire ceremony make it an overwhelming, unmissable experience.
shortPitch: As Hinduism's most sacred site, Varanasi is one of the world's oldest and most intense continuously inhabited cities. The ghats of the Ganges River serve as a raw, overwhelming theatre of spiritual existence and ancient ritual.
topHighlights: The mesmerising evening Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat
Dawn boat rides past ancient riverside ghats
Paying respects at the sacred Kashi Vishwanath Temple

name: Haridwar
description: Haridwar — 'Gateway to God' — is where the Ganges descends from the Himalayas onto the plains, making it one of the seven holiest cities in Hinduism. The evening Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri Ghat, where priests wave fire lamps over the river, is one of India's most moving spiritual spectacles.
shortPitch: Translating to 'Gateway to God', Haridwar marks the point where the Ganges descends from the Himalayas. It is a profoundly spiritual destination renowned for its evening river rituals and ancient temple traditions.
topHighlights: The grand evening Ganga Aarti at Har Ki Pauri Ghat
Ropeway trips to the hilltop Mansa Devi Temple
Witnessing the site of the legendary Kumbh Mela

name: Amritsar
description: Amritsar is the spiritual and cultural capital of Sikhism, home to the Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) — the holiest shrine in Sikhism and one of the most visited places in the world. The Jallianwala Bagh massacre memorial and the Wagah Border flag ceremony add profound historical dimensions.
shortPitch: Amritsar is the vibrant spiritual and cultural capital of Sikhism, universally famed for the radiant Golden Temple. It offers visitors profound historical depth, iconic ceremonies, and legendary Punjabi hospitality.
topHighlights: The awe-inspiring Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib)
The deeply moving Jallianwala Bagh Memorial
The electric daily sunset ceremony at the Wagah Border

name: Bodh Gaya
description: Bodh Gaya is the most sacred site in Buddhism — the place where Siddhartha Gautama attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree. The Mahabodhi Temple complex (UNESCO World Heritage) attracts Buddhist pilgrims from Tibet, Thailand, Sri Lanka, Japan, and around the world.
shortPitch: Bodh Gaya is revered globally as the most sacred site in Buddhism, where Siddhartha Gautama achieved enlightenment. The UNESCO-listed temple complex draws monks and pilgrims from across the world into deep, peaceful meditation.
topHighlights: The magnificent Mahabodhi Temple
Meditating beneath the descendant of the original Bodhi Tree
Exploring international monasteries from Japan, Tibet, and Thailand

name: Tirupati
description: The Tirumala Venkateswara Temple at Tirupati is the richest and most visited place of worship in the world, receiving 50,000–100,000 pilgrims daily. The main deity, Lord Venkateswara, is believed to grant all wishes, and the tradition of tonsuring (head shaving) as an offering is observed by millions.
shortPitch: Tirupati is home to the Tirumala Venkateswara Temple, renowned as the richest and most visited place of worship in the world. Ascending its sacred hills is an intense, awe-inspiring display of unwavering faith and devotion.
topHighlights: Darshan at the majestic Tirumala Venkateswara Temple
Historic visits to the nearby Chandragiri Fort
The beautiful cascading waters of Kapila Theertham

name: Puri
description: Puri is one of Hinduism's four sacred Dhams and home to the Jagannath Temple — one of India's most important shrines. The annual Rath Yatra chariot festival draws millions and is the world's largest chariot procession. The adjacent beach is one of Odisha's most popular seaside resorts.
shortPitch: Puri is an ancient coastal Dham deeply revered for the monumental Jagannath Temple. Alongside its vibrant spiritual rituals and the massive annual Rath Yatra, it offers expansive sandy beaches along the Bay of Bengal.
topHighlights: The highly sacred Jagannath Temple
Witnessing the colossal Rath Yatra Chariot Festival
Relaxing at the popular Puri Beach

name: Ajmer
description: Ajmer's Dargah Sharif — the shrine of Sufi saint Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti — is among the most important Muslim pilgrimage sites in South Asia, drawing devotees of all faiths. Just 11 km away, Pushkar is one of Hinduism's holiest sites, creating an extraordinary interfaith corridor.
shortPitch: Set in the Aravalli mountains, Ajmer is a deeply historic city and home to the universally revered Dargah Sharif shrine. Its beautiful lakes and Sufi heritage create a profound sense of interfaith devotion.
topHighlights: The sacred Dargah Sharif of Khwaja Moinuddin Chishti
Live Qawwali music performances at the dargah
Relaxing by the shores of Ana Sagar Lake

name: Shirdi
description: Shirdi is home to the Sai Baba Samadhi Mandir — one of the wealthiest temples in India and the most visited destination in Maharashtra. Millions of devotees from all religions visit annually to pay homage to Sai Baba, a saint revered by Hindus, Muslims and people of all faiths.
shortPitch: Shirdi is the sacred spiritual home of the widely revered saint Sai Baba. Drawing millions of devotees from every faith, the serene temple complex offers a deeply peaceful atmosphere centered around universal devotion.
topHighlights: Darshan at the main Sai Baba Samadhi Mandir
Visiting the historic Dwarkamai Mosque
Strolling through the peaceful Lendi Baugh garden

name: Jim Corbett National Park
description: Jim Corbett is India's oldest and most famous national park, established in 1936 and the flagship of Project Tiger. The Ramganga River flows through its sal forests, grasslands and river-side terrains, creating a diverse habitat. It holds one of the highest densities of Bengal tigers in India.
shortPitch: Established in 1936, Jim Corbett is India's oldest and most celebrated national park, serving as the pioneering flagship of Project Tiger. Its diverse landscape of sal forests and the Ramganga River provides thrilling safari experiences and incredible wildlife encounters.
topHighlights: Thrilling tiger safaris in the Dhikala and Bijrani zones
Birdwatching along the Ramganga River
Overnight stays in exclusive forest lodges under the stars

name: Ranthambore National Park
description: Ranthambore is India's most famous tiger reserve, where tigers are relatively habituated to vehicles — making it one of the best places in Asia for tiger photography. The dramatic backdrop of the 10th-century Ranthambore Fort rising above the jungle adds a unique historical dimension to wildlife safaris.
shortPitch: Set against the dramatic backdrop of a 10th-century fort, Ranthambore is renowned for its habituated tigers, making it Asia's premier destination for wildlife photography. It offers an unparalleled blend of ancient history and thrilling jungle safaris.
topHighlights: Unforgettable tiger sightings in an open jeep or canter
Exploring the ancient, UNESCO-listed Ranthambore Fort inside the park
Spotting crocodiles and birds at Padam Talao lake

name: Kaziranga National Park
description: Kaziranga is a UNESCO World Heritage Site holding two-thirds of the world's endangered one-horned rhinoceros population. Its vast floodplain grasslands along the Brahmaputra River also shelter the highest density of tigers in any protected area in the world, alongside wild elephants, water buffalo and swamp deer.
shortPitch: This UNESCO World Heritage Site is a vast floodplain along the Brahmaputra River, proudly sheltering two-thirds of the world's endangered one-horned rhinos. It promises an extraordinary safari experience with incredible densities of tigers, elephants, and unique endemic species.
topHighlights: Spotting the majestic one-horned rhinoceros on jeep safaris
Early morning elephant safaris through the tall grasslands
Boat safaris along the Brahmaputra spotting diverse birdlife

name: Bandhavgarh National Park
description: Bandhavgarh has one of the highest densities of Bengal tigers in India and is consistently rated among the best parks for tiger sightings. The ancient Bandhavgarh Fort with its giant reclining Vishnu statue adds an archaeological dimension to the wildlife experience.
shortPitch: Boasting one of India's highest Bengal tiger densities, Bandhavgarh consistently offers some of the best big cat sightings in the world. The experience is elevated by ancient cave temples and a majestic fort hidden deep within the lush jungle.
topHighlights: Exceptional tiger tracking safaris in the Tala zone
Discovering the ancient Bandhavgarh Fort and cave shrines
Searching for leopards, sloth bears, and rich birdlife

name: Kanha National Park
description: Kanha inspired Rudyard Kipling's 'The Jungle Book' and remains one of India's finest wildlife sanctuaries. Its vast meadows (maidans) dotted with sal and bamboo forests are prime tiger country, while Kanha is also credited with saving the barasingha (swamp deer) from extinction.
shortPitch: The majestic inspiration behind 'The Jungle Book', Kanha's sprawling meadows and dense sal forests are a quintessential Indian wildlife experience. It is a premier tiger habitat and famously the last stronghold of the rare barasingha swamp deer.
topHighlights: Tiger and barasingha spotting in the iconic Kanha meadows
Witnessing stunning sunsets from the Bamni Dadar viewpoint
Immersing in the real-life landscapes of 'The Jungle Book'

name: Pench National Park
description: Pench — another Jungle Book landscape — straddles Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra, offering superb wildlife in less crowded conditions than Kanha or Bandhavgarh. It is famous for its leopards, wild dogs, and the tiger named 'Collarwali' — one of the most prolific and studied tigresses in India.
shortPitch: Straddling two states, Pench offers incredible, less-crowded wildlife safaris through teak forests immortalized in Kipling's tales. It is celebrated for its thriving populations of leopards, wild dogs, and legendary tigers.
topHighlights: Tracking tigers and elusive leopards on guided jeep safaris
Scenic boat safaris along the peaceful Pench River
Observing wild dog packs in their natural habitat

name: Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve
description: Tadoba is Maharashtra's oldest and largest national park, gaining fame as a tiger hotspot with excellent sighting rates. Its teak forests, bamboo groves and Tadoba Lake make it visually dramatic, and the resident tigers — including the celebrated Matkasur family — are well studied and habituated.
shortPitch: Maharashtra's oldest national park is a rising star in Indian wildlife, offering phenomenal tiger sighting rates amidst dramatic teak and bamboo forests. The beautiful Tadoba Lake acts as an oasis for leopards, gaur, and crocodiles.
topHighlights: Consistent and thrilling tiger sightings on guided safaris
Wildlife viewing by the scenic Tadoba Lake
Spotting Indian bison (gaur) and sloth bears in the dense forests

name: Gir Forest National Park
description: Gir is the last wild habitat of the Asiatic lion — found nowhere else on Earth outside Africa. The park's dry deciduous forests in Saurashtra shelter around 600 lions, plus leopards, hyenas and the largest population of crocodiles in Gujarat. Lion safari is among India's most exclusive wildlife experiences.
shortPitch: Gir is globally unique as the absolute last refuge on Earth for the majestic Asiatic lion. Its dry deciduous forests offer an incredibly exclusive safari experience, alongside rich populations of leopards, hyenas, and crocodiles.
topHighlights: Exclusive open-jeep safaris to spot the rare Asiatic lion
Birdwatching and wildlife photography in dry deciduous forests
Spotting marsh crocodiles at Kamleshwar Dam

name: Sundarbans
description: The Sundarbans is the world's largest mangrove forest (10,000 sq km) and a UNESCO World Heritage Site, straddling India and Bangladesh. It is the only place in the world where Bengal tigers live in a mangrove tidal delta ecosystem — these 'swimming tigers' are uniquely adapted to the saltwater environment.
shortPitch: The world's largest mangrove forest is a mystical UNESCO World Heritage Site, famous for its elusive, swimming Bengal tigers. Exploring its intricate tidal waterways by boat offers a hauntingly beautiful and totally unique wildlife experience.
topHighlights: Boat safaris through winding, mysterious mangrove creeks
Seeking the legendary 'swimming tigers' from watchtowers
Experiencing the Dobsonki Camp canopy walk

name: Nagarhole & Bandipur (Nilgiri Biosphere)
description: Nagarhole (Rajiv Gandhi National Park) and adjacent Bandipur together form the core of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve — India's largest tiger conservation area. The contiguous forests here hold the highest concentration of Asian elephants in the world, alongside tigers, leopards and the endemic Nilgiri tahr.
shortPitch: Forming the heart of the Nilgiri Biosphere, these contiguous forests hold the world's highest concentration of Asian elephants. The lush landscape, framed by the Kabini River, provides world-class sightings of tigers, leopards, and wild dog packs.
topHighlights: Witnessing massive herds of Asian elephants on safari
Sunset boat rides and wildlife viewing on the Kabini River
Luxury stays at eco-lodges beneath the starry forest sky

name: Periyar Tiger Reserve
description: Periyar, in the heart of Thekkady's spice hills, is one of India's most accessible and scenic wildlife reserves. Boat rides on the Periyar Lake offer relaxed wildlife watching — herds of elephants, sambar deer and otters come to the water's edge — while the surrounding tea and cardamom estates perfume the air.
shortPitch: Nestled in Kerala's fragrant spice hills, Periyar offers exceptionally scenic and relaxed wildlife viewing from the waters of its central lake. It is a haven for elephant herds, lush nature walks, and nearby aromatic cardamom estates.
topHighlights: Relaxing boat safaris on Periyar Lake watching wild elephants
Bamboo rafting and guided tribal border hikes
Exploring the nearby vibrant Kumily spice markets

name: Munnar
description: Munnar is Kerala's premier hill station, its valleys blanketed in endless rolling tea estates at 1,600 m. The rare Neelakurinji flower — which blooms only once every 12 years, turning the hillsides purple — is Munnar's most celebrated natural phenomenon. Misty mornings, wildlife and cardamom forests add to its appeal.
shortPitch: Kerala's premier hill station is a breathtaking canvas of endless rolling tea estates wrapped in cool mountain mists. It offers serene plantation walks, spectacular viewpoints, and tranquil escapes into high-altitude nature.
topHighlights: Driving through endless, meticulously manicured tea plantations
Spotting the endangered Nilgiri tahr at Eravikulam National Park
Enjoying panoramic views from Top Station and Echo Point

name: Darjeeling
description: Darjeeling, at 2,042 m in the Eastern Himalayas, is synonymous with the world's finest teas. The Toy Train (UNESCO World Heritage), views of the Kanchenjunga, colonial-era clubs and the famous sunrise from Tiger Hill make it India's most beloved hill station. Darjeeling's FTGFOP1 first-flush teas are liquid gold.
shortPitch: Synonymous with the world's finest teas, Darjeeling is a timeless Himalayan retreat offering majestic views of Mount Kanchenjunga. The vintage UNESCO Toy Train and colonial-era charm make it deeply nostalgic and romantic.
topHighlights: Watching the sunrise over Kanchenjunga from Tiger Hill
Riding the historic UNESCO Darjeeling Himalayan Railway
Touring legendary tea estates and tasting first-flush brews

name: Ooty (Udhagamandalam)
description: Ooty is the 'Queen of Hill Stations' — the most famous hill resort in South India. The Nilgiri Mountain Railway (Toy Train) UNESCO heritage line, botanical gardens, the Ooty Lake and surrounding eucalyptus and tea estates have drawn visitors since the British Raj.
shortPitch: The undisputed 'Queen of Hill Stations', Ooty enchants with its sprawling botanical gardens, scenic lakes, and crisp eucalyptus air. The nostalgic ride on its UNESCO-listed mountain railway remains a quintessential Indian experience.
topHighlights: Riding the UNESCO Nilgiri Mountain Railway toy train
Strolling through the meticulously maintained Ooty Botanical Gardens
Boating on the serene waters of Ooty Lake

name: Coonoor
description: Coonoor is quieter and less crowded than Ooty but equally beautiful, with terraced tea gardens cascading down the Nilgiri slopes. Sim's Park is a magnificent botanical garden, and the town serves as the main gateway for the Nilgiri Mountain Railway experience between Mettupalayam and Ooty.
shortPitch: A quieter, more intimate neighbor to Ooty, Coonoor features breathtaking terraced tea gardens cascading down the Nilgiri hills. It offers stunning viewpoints, peaceful botanical parks, and a slower, deeply relaxing pace of life.
topHighlights: Wandering through the rare flora at Sim's Park
Taking in valley panoramas from Dolphin's Nose viewpoint
Enjoying peaceful drives through cascading tea estates

name: Delhi
description: Delhi, India's capital, layers 3,000 years of history from the Mughal Empire to the modern Republic. Old Delhi's lanes around the Red Fort and Jama Masjid contrast sharply with Lutyens' Delhi's grand colonial boulevards and New Delhi's diplomatic enclaves. It is the gateway for millions of international travellers.
shortPitch: India's pulsing capital perfectly bridges the ancient and the modern, layering 3,000 years of history alongside cutting-edge culture. From the chaotic charm of Old Delhi to grand colonial boulevards, it is a city of extraordinary depth.
topHighlights: Exploring UNESCO heritage at the Red Fort and Qutub Minar
Navigating the vibrant, spice-scented lanes of Chandni Chowk
Admiring the Mughal architectural perfection of Humayun's Tomb

name: Mumbai
description: Mumbai — the 'City of Dreams' — is India's financial capital, Bollywood's home and a city of extraordinary contrasts. Its Gothic colonial architecture, Art Deco seafront, island forts, bustling street food and Asia's largest slum Dharavi sit alongside luxury neighbourhoods and one of the world's most vibrant arts scenes.
shortPitch: The relentless 'City of Dreams' is India's financial and entertainment powerhouse, offering a dizzying mix of Bollywood glamour and street-level grit. Its stunning Gothic architecture and seaside promenades define its iconic coastal skyline.
topHighlights: Strolling the seafront promenade of Marine Drive at sunset
Marveling at the Gateway of India and taking a ferry to Elephanta Caves
Taking an insightful, community-led walking tour of Dharavi

name: Bengaluru (Bangalore)
description: Bengaluru is India's Silicon Valley and the fastest-growing major city in Asia. Known for its pleasant climate, startup culture, craft beer scene, colonial parks and vibrant arts communities, it is the modern face of India's economic rise — a cosmopolitan city that blends technology, tradition and progressive culture.
shortPitch: India's thriving Silicon Valley blends cutting-edge startup culture with magnificent colonial parks and a world-class craft beer scene. Blessed with a perpetually pleasant climate, it is the progressive, modern face of the nation.
topHighlights: Relaxing among ancient trees in Lalbagh Botanical Garden
Exploring the city's famous craft brewery and nightlife scene
Visiting the majestic, Tudor-style Bengaluru Palace

name: Kolkata
description: Kolkata — the 'City of Joy' and former British capital of India — is the cultural and intellectual heart of the nation. Its crumbling colonial grandeur, world-class art scene, Durga Puja festival, hand-pulled rickshaws, Tagore's Rabindranath heritage and legendary Bengali cuisine make it one of India's most richly textured cities.
shortPitch: The former British capital remains India's profound intellectual and cultural heart, radiating old-world colonial grandeur. Famous for its literary heritage and legendary street food, Kolkata is a deeply soulful and textured 'City of Joy'.
topHighlights: Admiring the imposing marble grandeur of the Victoria Memorial
Crossing the iconic Howrah Bridge over the Hooghly River
Experiencing the unparalleled energy of the Durga Puja festival

name: Chennai
description: Chennai is the capital of Tamil Nadu and the gateway to South India. It is India's fourth-largest city and the centre of classical Carnatic music, Bharatanatyam dance, ancient Dravidian temple architecture and South Indian cinema. Marina Beach — the world's second-longest urban beach — runs the length of the city.
shortPitch: The cultural gateway to South India, Chennai beautifully balances deep-rooted Dravidian traditions with the energy of a massive metropolis. It is the heart of classical Carnatic music, ancient temples, and the sprawling sands of Marina Beach.
topHighlights: Strolling along the incredibly vast Marina Beach
Marveling at the ancient, towering Kapaleeshwarar Temple
Experiencing an authentic Bharatanatyam classical dance performance

name: Hyderabad
description: Hyderabad — the 'City of Pearls' — blends Mughal grandeur with modern IT prosperity. The Charminar, Golconda Fort, pearl and bangles bazaars, dum biryani and the Nizam's lavish palaces create a city of extraordinary historical depth and culinary richness.
shortPitch: The dynamic 'City of Pearls' seamlessly fuses Mughal royal grandeur with booming modern prosperity. It offers world-famous culinary delights like dum biryani, set against the backdrop of iconic monuments like the Charminar and Golconda Fort.
topHighlights: Admiring the 400-year-old architectural marvel, the Charminar
Exploring the imposing ruins and acoustics of Golconda Fort
Shopping for pearls and bangles at the bustling Laad Bazaar

name: Shillong
description: Shillong — the 'Scotland of the East' — is the capital of Meghalaya and the cultural hub of the Northeast, famous for its pine-covered rolling hills, British-era architecture, thriving music scene and proximity to Cherrapunji. It is one of India's few cities where rock music is a cultural institution.
shortPitch: Affectionately dubbed the 'Scotland of the East', Shillong is a misty, pine-covered hill station that serves as the cultural heart of the Northeast. It boasts a thriving rock music scene, colonial-era charm, and incredibly hospitable locals.
topHighlights: Enjoying a boat ride on the scenic, serene Ward's Lake
Taking in panoramic valley views from Shillong Peak
Experiencing the city's legendary live indie and rock music scene

name: Cherrapunji & Mawsynram
description: Cherrapunji (Sohra) and nearby Mawsynram are the wettest places on Earth — receiving over 11,000 mm of rainfall annually. The result is a world of extraordinary waterfalls, living root bridges grown by the Khasi people over centuries, emerald gorges and mist-shrouded forests of unparalleled lushness.
shortPitch: Officially the wettest places on Earth, this region is a breathtaking wonderland of emerald gorges and plunging waterfalls. It is famous for the ingenious, centuries-old living root bridges woven by the indigenous Khasi people.
topHighlights: Trekking to the astonishing Double-Decker Living Root Bridge
Marveling at the colossal Nohkalikai Falls plunging into the gorge
Exploring the deep, mystical limestone networks of Mawsmai Caves

name: Mawlynnong
description: Mawlynnong is known as the 'Cleanest Village in Asia' — a title it has held with extraordinary pride. This Khasi village, near the Bangladesh border, is famous for its immaculate bamboo paths, sky-view tree platforms, community spirit and the nearby Dawki River's impossibly transparent water.
shortPitch: Proudly recognized as the 'Cleanest Village in Asia', this immaculate Khasi settlement is a triumph of community-driven eco-tourism. Visitors wander bamboo-lined paths, scale sky-view treehouse platforms, and experience perfect harmony with nature.
topHighlights: Strolling the impeccably clean, flower-lined village pathways
Climbing the bamboo Sky Walk for views into Bangladesh
Experiencing authentic local Khasi meals and community hospitality

name: Dawki & Umngot River
description: Dawki on the Umngot River is one of India's most photographed destinations — the river's extraordinary clarity makes boats appear to float on glass. The annual Dawki boat race is a colourful Khasi tradition, and the Bangladesh border bridge is a fascinating cultural crossing point.
shortPitch: Dawki is home to the phenomenally clear Umngot River, where the waters are so transparent that boats appear to be floating on thin air. This stunning border town offers surreal photography opportunities and peaceful river escapes.
topHighlights: Gliding on a traditional wooden boat over the crystal-clear Umngot River
Crossing the historic Dawki Suspension Bridge built by the British
Visiting the fascinating India-Bangladesh border crossing at Tamabil

name: Majuli Island
description: Majuli is the world's largest river island (on the Brahmaputra River) and a UNESCO World Heritage candidate. It is the spiritual and cultural heartland of Assamese Neo-Vaishnavism, with ancient Satras (monasteries) preserving mask-making, classical dance and weaving traditions unique to Assam.
shortPitch: Resting on the mighty Brahmaputra, Majuli is the world's largest river island and the spiritual epicenter of Assamese culture. It is a tranquil sanctuary of ancient monasteries, unique mask-making artisans, and incredible migratory birdlife.
topHighlights: Visiting the ancient Satras (Vaishnavite monasteries) and monks
Witnessing the intricate, traditional Assamese mask-making crafts
Cycling through peaceful, scenic Mishing tribal farming villages

name: Nagaland & Hornbill Festival
description: Nagaland's Hornbill Festival — held every December in Kisama — is the most vibrant tribal cultural festival in India, showcasing the traditions of 17 Naga tribes in one breathtaking venue. Warrior dances, traditional foods, log drums, indigenous crafts and headhunting history make Nagaland utterly unique.
shortPitch: The host of the legendary Hornbill Festival, Nagaland is a breathtaking tapestry of indigenous warrior culture and untamed nature. Its incredibly vibrant festivals, log drum beats, and fascinating headhunting history offer an unparalleled cultural immersion.
topHighlights: Witnessing the mesmerizing dances and costumes at the Hornbill Festival
Paying respects at the meticulously maintained WWII Kohima War Cemetery
Trekking the rolling, emerald-green hills of the Dzükou Valley

name: Hampi
description: Hampi is one of India's most extraordinary archaeological sites — the ruined capital of the Vijayanagara Empire (14th–16th century) scattered across a surreal landscape of giant boulders, banana plantations and the Tungabhadra River. Corinthian temples, elephant stables and market streets speak of a civilisation of immense wealth and sophistication.
shortPitch: Set amidst a surreal, otherworldly landscape of giant balancing boulders and lush banana plantations, Hampi is the spectacular ruined capital of the Vijayanagara Empire. These UNESCO-listed stone temples and palaces feel like stepping onto another planet.
topHighlights: Exploring the intricate musical pillars of the Vittala Temple
Hiking up Matanga Hill for an unforgettable boulder-strewn sunrise
Cycling past ancient elephant stables and bustling medieval bazaars

name: Khajuraho
description: Khajuraho's Chandela temples (10th–11th century) are among the finest medieval temple complexes in the world, celebrated for their exquisite erotic sculptures representing all aspects of human life. The UNESCO World Heritage temples are masterpieces of Nagara-style architecture.
shortPitch: Renowned globally for its astonishingly intricate and sensuous sculptures, Khajuraho is a masterpiece of 10th-century Chandela temple architecture. This UNESCO World Heritage site is a stunning celebration of medieval art, spirituality, and human life.
topHighlights: Admiring the incredibly detailed carvings of the Western Temple Group
Attending the enchanting evening Sound & Light Show in the temple gardens
Combining culture with wildlife by visiting the nearby Panna Tiger Reserve

name: Mahabalipuram (Mamallapuram)
description: Mahabalipuram on the Coromandel Coast is a UNESCO World Heritage Site containing some of the finest examples of Pallava rock-cut and structural architecture. The Shore Temple, the Five Rathas and the giant bas-relief of Arjuna's Penance are masterpieces carved directly from coastal rock faces 1,300 years ago.
shortPitch: Perched on the breezy Coromandel Coast, this UNESCO World Heritage town showcases absolute masterworks of 7th-century Pallava rock-cut architecture. It perfectly blends dramatic oceanside ruins, monumental bas-reliefs, and a relaxed beachside atmosphere.
topHighlights: Visiting the iconic Shore Temple, standing majestically against the ocean
Marveling at Arjuna's Penance, the world's largest open-air bas-relief
Exploring the incredible monolithic rock-cut shrines of the Five Rathas

name: Konark Sun Temple
description: The Konark Sun Temple is one of India's greatest architectural achievements — a 13th-century stone chariot for the Sun God with 24 elaborately carved wheels and 7 galloping horses, built on an enormous scale. The temple's erotic sculptures, Odishan dance festival and coastal location make it a remarkable destination.
shortPitch: Conceived as a colossal, 13th-century stone chariot for the Sun God, Konark is one of India's most staggering architectural triumphs. The intricately carved gigantic stone wheels and the incredible scale of this UNESCO site leave visitors awestruck.
topHighlights: Studying the incredibly detailed stone wheels and erotic sculptures
Attending the vibrant Konark Dance Festival held against the ruins
Relaxing on the nearby serene shores of Chandrabhaga Beach

name: Ajanta & Ellora Caves
description: Ajanta's 29 Buddhist cave monasteries (2nd century BC–6th century AD) contain the finest surviving examples of ancient Indian painting in the world. Ellora's 34 caves encompass Buddhist, Hindu and Jain temples carved from volcanic rock — including the monolithic Kailasa Temple, the world's largest rock-cut structure.
shortPitch: Carved directly into solid volcanic cliffs, these dual UNESCO World Heritage sites represent the pinnacle of ancient Indian rock-cut architecture. While Ajanta harbors the world's finest ancient Buddhist murals, Ellora boasts the mind-boggling monolithic Kailasa Temple.
topHighlights: Standing awestruck inside the monolithic Kailasa Temple at Ellora
Viewing the exquisitely preserved ancient Buddhist frescoes at Ajanta
Exploring the fascinating mix of Hindu, Buddhist, and Jain cave shrines

name: Orchha
description: Orchha is a remarkably well-preserved medieval Bundela capital on the Betwa River, whose temples, cenotaphs, palaces and chhatris rise from the banks like a forgotten dream. Less visited than Rajasthan's forts yet equally beautiful, Orchha is a top recommendation for heritage travellers seeking an authentic, uncrowded experience.
shortPitch: Rising like a forgotten dream from the banks of the Betwa River, Orchha is a perfectly preserved, atmospheric medieval capital. Free from mass tourism crowds, its towering palaces and riverside cenotaphs offer a deeply romantic and authentic heritage experience.
topHighlights: Wandering through the magnificent empty courtyards of Jehangir Mahal
Watching the sunset over the riverside royal chhatris (cenotaphs)
Experiencing the evening aarti at the unique Ram Raja Palace-Temple

name: Madurai
description: Madurai is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world and the temple city of Tamil Nadu. The Meenakshi Amman Temple — with its soaring gopurams encrusted in thousands of sculptures — is among the most spectacular religious complexes in Asia, active 24 hours a day.
shortPitch: One of the oldest continuously inhabited cities on Earth, Madurai is the beating, spiritual heart of Tamil Nadu. Its epicenter is the colossal Meenakshi Amman Temple, a breathtaking labyrinth of wildly colorful, sculpture-encrusted towers.
topHighlights: Getting lost in the mesmerizing, 24-hour Meenakshi Amman Temple complex
Admiring the Indo-Saracenic grandeur of the Thirumalai Nayak Palace
Exploring the vibrant, chaotic, and ancient temple bazaars

name: Kochi (Cochin)
description: Kochi is Kerala's most cosmopolitan city — a multicultural port with Chinese fishing nets, a 500-year-old synagogue, Portuguese churches, Dutch palaces, colonial bungalows and one of India's most exciting contemporary art scenes (Kochi-Muziris Biennale). Fort Kochi's waterfront is quintessential romantic India.
shortPitch: Kerala's most cosmopolitan port city is an incredibly romantic mosaic of Portuguese, Dutch, Jewish, and British colonial heritage. The atmospheric streets of Fort Kochi buzz with trendy cafes, antique shops, and a thriving contemporary arts scene.
topHighlights: Watching the sunset through the iconic, cantilevered Chinese Fishing Nets
Strolling the quaint, heritage-rich streets of Jewish Town in Mattancherry
Experiencing the vibrant, world-class Kochi-Muziris Biennale arts festival

name: Dwarka & Somnath
description: Dwarka is one of the four sacred Hindu Dhams and the legendary kingdom of Lord Krishna. The Dwarkadhish Temple sits at the edge of the Arabian Sea. Nearby Somnath holds the first of 12 Jyotirlinga shrines of Lord Shiva, rebuilt numerous times after Islamic invasions and now a symbol of Hindu resilience.
shortPitch: Perched dramatically on the Arabian Sea, this deeply sacred coastal circuit links Lord Krishna's legendary kingdom of Dwarka with Somnath. Somnath's towering oceanfront temple is a breathtaking symbol of architectural resilience and spiritual devotion.
topHighlights: Witnessing the powerful crashing waves against the Somnath Jyotirlinga Temple
Taking a ferry across the sea to the ancient island of Bet Dwarka
Experiencing the vibrant evening aarti at the Dwarkadhish Temple

name: Tiruvannamalai
description: Tiruvannamalai is one of Tamil Nadu's most spiritually potent destinations — home to Arunachaleswarar Temple (dedicated to Shiva as fire element) and the sacred Arunachala Hill. The Girivalam — a barefoot circumambulation of the hill — is performed by hundreds of thousands every full moon night.
shortPitch: A deeply potent spiritual epicenter in South India, Tiruvannamalai is dominated by the sacred Arunachala Hill and its colossal Shiva temple. It draws seekers from around the world to meditate in the ashram of Ramana Maharshi.
topHighlights: Walking the sacred Girivalam barefoot path around Arunachala Hill
Meditating in the peaceful, globally renowned Ramana Maharshi Ashram
Exploring the massive courtyards of the Arunachaleswarar Temple

name: Vrindavan & Mathura
description: Mathura is the birthplace of Lord Krishna and Vrindavan the place of his childhood divine play (leela). The twin cities on the Yamuna River are among Hinduism's most sacred sites, with hundreds of colourful temples, the Holi festival (celebrated most exuberantly here) and year-round pilgrimage traffic.
shortPitch: The vibrant twin cities on the Yamuna River are the joyful epicenters of Krishna devotion. Boasting hundreds of colourful temples, this sacred corridor famously erupts into the most spectacular, exuberant Holi festival celebration in the world.
topHighlights: Experiencing the intensely colorful and chaotic Holi festival (in spring)
Visiting the sacred birthplace of Krishna at the Janmabhoomi Temple
Attending the mesmerizing evening aarti at the Banke Bihari Temple

name: Lonar Crater Lake
description: Lonar is a meteor-impact crater lake 50,000 years old — the third largest natural saline crater lake in the world. The lake's hyper-saline alkaline waters harbour unique pink algae and rare micro-organisms, while ancient temples ring the crater rim in a hauntingly beautiful forest setting.
shortPitch: Formed by a massive meteor impact 50,000 years ago, Lonar is a hauntingly beautiful, hyper-saline crater lake. Ringed by a lush forest and ancient, forgotten temples, it is a uniquely fascinating geological and historical wonder.
topHighlights: Trekking down into the 50,000-year-old meteor impact crater
Discovering the ancient, intricately carved Daitya Sudan Temple
Spotting migratory flamingos on the lake's unique pinkish waters

name: Chettinad
description: Chettinad is the homeland of the Nattukotai Chettiar merchant community, famous for their palatial mansions (with Burma teak, Italian marble and Athangudi tiles), the most complex regional cuisine in India, and antique collecting traditions. The crumbling palaces of Karaikudi and Kanadukathan are a singular architectural heritage.
shortPitch: Once the homeland of incredibly wealthy merchants, Chettinad is a surreal landscape of crumbling, palatial mansions built with Burmese teak and Italian marble. It is a haven for architecture lovers, antique collectors, and gourmands.
topHighlights: Wandering through massive, extravagantly decorated 19th-century mansions
Tasting the intensely flavorful, world-renowned authentic Chettinad cuisine
Hunting for unique vintage treasures in Karaikudi's antique markets

name: Unakoti
description: Unakoti — meaning 'one less than a crore' — is a stunning hillside complex of giant rock carvings and sculptures dating to the 7th–9th centuries, hidden deep in the forests of Tripura. The massive 30-foot Shiva heads emerging from the jungle are among the most dramatic archaeological discoveries in Northeast India.
shortPitch: Hidden deep in the jungles of Tripura, Unakoti is a stunning, mysterious hillside covered in massive rock carvings from the 7th century. The giant, 30-foot stone faces of Shiva emerging from the forest are a dramatic, undiscovered marvel.
topHighlights: Hiking through the lush jungle trail to discover giant rock-cut sculptures
Standing in awe before the massive, 30-foot central Shiva head
Cooling off by the picturesque, cascading Unakoti waterfall

name: Aizawl & Mizoram
description: Mizoram — one of India's least visited states — is a land of rolling bamboo-covered hills, deeply Christian culture, thriving choir traditions and one of India's highest literacy rates. Aizawl perches dramatically on a ridge at 1,132 m, and the Mizo people are among the most hospitable and unique communities in the subcontinent.
shortPitch: One of India's most remote and least-visited states, Mizoram is a rolling landscape of bamboo forests and deeply hospitable, choir-singing communities. The capital Aizawl sits dramatically on a high ridge, offering incredible serenity and cultural immersion.
topHighlights: Enjoying sweeping, panoramic views from the high-ridge city of Aizawl
Attending the vibrant, bamboo-focused Chapchar Kut spring festival
Exploring the pristine, untamed forests of Phawngpui (Blue Mountain)

name: Jawai
description: Exclusive luxury leopard safaris amidst striking granite hills.
shortPitch: A stark, dramatic landscape in Rajasthan where leopards roam freely among massive granite boulders. Jawai offers a unique, luxurious wilderness experience intertwined with local Rabari herdsmen.
topHighlights: Tracking wild leopards thriving in the rocky granite hills
Staying in ultra-luxury, sustainable wilderness camps
Interacting with the indigenous Rabari pastoralists

name: Munroe Island
description: Untouched backwater network, perfect for quiet canoe explorations.
shortPitch: A hidden gem in the Kerala backwaters, Munroe Island offers an intimate, untouched network of narrow canals. It is perfect for quiet canoe explorations away from the commercial houseboat routes.
topHighlights: Taking a silent, sunrise canoe ride through narrow mangrove canals
Watching traditional coir weaving in a local village
Enjoying the absolute tranquility of untouched backwaters
